description: The animate quality that distinguishes living beings from inanimate matter; the vital force that flows through all creatures. 'Lioru' names what quickens—the difference between breathing and still.
sound_symbolism: The word flows with vitality. 'Li' lifts with light energy, life rising. 'O' opens with breathing fullness. Then 'ru' runs with continuing movement, life persisting.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo lioru' (the life). The force animating 'miona' (person), 'thenu' (animal). Opposite of death/stillness.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Life as precious—this human birth, rare and fleeting, to be honored.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Life as interconnected web—all beings part of living systems, none separate.
